summary: This ERC provides a standard for signing multiple typed-data messages with a single signature by encoding them into a Merkle tree. This allows components to independently verify messages, without requiring full knowledge of the others. It provides a significant UX improvement by reducing the number of signature prompts to one, while preserving the security and flexibility of the [EIP-712](./eip-712.md) standard. This ERC also gives applications the flexibility to verify messages in isolation, or in aggregate. This opens up new verification modalities: for e.g, an application can require that message (`x`) is only valid when signed in combination message (`y`).
title: Composite EIP-712 Signatures
